reviews.title: Watch is good, not great with mybphone
reviews.text:
The watch is great. Synched quickly with the app and I like the number of different face options offered. The battery life is also strong and it charges quickly.
Things I didn't like. If you touch the face it quickly unlocks and scrolls through the menus. So when I cross my arms I can mess up the watch, it needs a better lock. The app didn't synch all options to my watch/phone. So, I couldn't track sleep, heartrate and other things.
Maybe if I have a better phone?

reviews.title: Kids love it
reviews.text: It is a generic version of a smart watch but the kids don’t realize it as it works almost exactly as the real thing. It tells the time, lets you make and receive calls and text and you download the app just like you would with the real thing. It’s easy to set up. Comes with a charging cord. Connects to any phone. #FoundItOnAmazon

reviews.title: Great Apple alternative!
reviews.text: Got this for my mom, here it is next to my Apple Watch, it has pretty much all the same features as the Apple at about a quarter of the price. The battery lasts all day and seems to be on par with the battery life of my watch. The app is easy to use and my mom loves it!

reviews.title: Awesome Smart Watch
reviews.text:
For the money, this is an awesome smart watch that has some of the same popular features as that “fruit” brand.
The watch was packaged securely in a box and included a User Manual and a “How to Connect with the smart watch” brochure.

My watch was received with 46% battery life and did not need to be charged immediately, but I noticed that the charge cord is magnetic and attaches easily to the back of the watch.

I read the instructions and powered on the watch. I downloaded the app and proceeded to pair, referred to as “bind” in the manual. The smart watch paired effortlessly with my iPhone 13. Other than pairing or unpairing, the user manual doesn’t cover much else, but I found the watch use to be intuitive. I do think a more detailed user manual would be helpful to explain navigating and swipe direction of the screens.

The watch has many functions and I was pleasantly surprised that the watch provided a “Siri connection” and performed flawlessly. I compared the blood pressure and oxygen sensors to my individual monitors to check accuracy. Surprisingly, the watch is pretty accurate.

As I mentioned earlier, I started using the watch with 46% battery life and wore the watch for approximately 25 hours before I received a low battery alert.

This is a great buy and so much so, that within hours of using my watch, I ordered another one for my husband.

reviews.title: Best watch yet!
reviews.text: I'm very impressed with this smart watch. It has so many features and works well. Definitely recommend it.

features: 【Fitness Tracker & Blood Oxygen Monitor】 GRV N29 smartwatch tracks your daily steps, heart rate, calorie, sleep quality and distance throughout the day, and records your activity data in the app. Contains 10 different sports modes: indoor walking, outdoor walking, indoor running, outdoor running, outdoor cycling, yoga, spinning, boating, etc. You can measure the body's blood oxygen saturation after exercise or in normal times.

The watch has a waterproof rating of IP68, which makes it shower proof and wearable in a swimming pool. It would be safe even to submerge up to a depth of 1.5 metres for around half an hour. The strap is changeable and made of soft silicone, very comfortable.

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

reviews.title: Impressive watch with features
reviews.text: Watch has many features for fitness and heath I bought 2. Some of the screens are well thought out. Accuracy is a key benefit of this watch. Needed to use newer version of app and works with my android phone. Current version is metric and new version is to have imperial values. Vendor was responsive and resolved questions ASAP. Plan to buy new version with upgrades when available

reviews.title: lastima el software del celular
reviews.text:
Este reloj tiene varias cosas interesantes, pero lastimosamente el software del celular que lo controla tiene muchas fallas, por ejemplo cuando uno agrega una lista de contactos favoritos al reloj, esporadicamente desaparece y hay que volver a crearla. Tambien es frecuente que se desconecta el programa y el reloj, por lo que se dejan de recibir notificaciones en el reloj.

Tambien hay problemas con la personalizacion del skin, donde uno selecciona una foto de fondo, la descarga al reloj y aveces despues de unos minutos u horas desaparece y toca volver a hacer el proceso.

Tambien la aplicacion del celular esta mal diseñada, algunos botones no se ven bien y es dificil encontrar como seguir cuando se quiere configurar el reloj.

Lo bueno del reloj:
- permite recibir y hacer llamadas desde el reloj ya que trae microfono y un pequeño parlante, pero hay que aclarar que necesita al celular cerca y conectado por bluetooth.
- Reporta bien la mayoria de notificaciones que recive el celular, como llegada de SMS, emails, mensajes de whatsapp, aunque no muestra emojins.

-La bateria carga en 90minutos completamente y puede durar 3 dias sin tener que recargar.

En resumen hasta que el fabricante no mejore los problemas de software no es una buena compra este reloj.

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: It’s ok

reviews.title: I wanted to love it
reviews.text: It really isn't the watches fault that I returned it.  It did all it said it would do, the Alexa feature worked, the heart rate monitor worked , the picture was bright, among other things. I didn't like that if you use the timer ( which I use frequently at my job) you can't go back to the main screen, if you do it turns off the timer, but you can use Alexa as a timer and don't have to stay on the same screen so there is a work around. I was also dissapointed with the fact there were only 4 face choices.  But, the reason I returned it is that my phone is older and it wears down my battery quickly and was causing cpu (?) overload warnings.

reviews.title: Solid watch
reviews.text:
Watch came well packaged and was easy to set up and pair using the provided instructions on the item page. It was made well and kept good time. I have a Motorola phone and it would not stay paired with my phone. The sleep feature never seemed to work because it was constantly disconnecting from the app. It may be a good choice for someone else, but I returned it.

The battery life was excellent and it was a well constructed and comfortable watch

reviews.title: Easier to set-up than previous bands
reviews.text:
I have really struggled getting replacement bands in place in the past. I had this one switched over in a matter of minutes. The lacy look makes it less apparent that the band is vinyl and the dark colors help to hide stains. I have bad metal allergies nd was a bit afraid of what would happen with the clasp given it is metal and has some contact with my skin. Been wearing this since December and the only time I have issues with a rash was when I had the watch fastened too tightly to my wrist. I believe this was from trapped sweat and moisture and not a metal allergy reaction.
Extra great deal in that you get three bands. I don't foresee a need to buy more bands for at least a year.

title: Garmin 010-02172-31 Vivoactive 4S, Smaller-Sized GPS Smartwatch, Features Music, Body Energy Monitoring, Animated Workouts, Pulse Ox Sensors and More, Light Gold with Light Pink Band
description: Tune in to your body, and tone up with the 40 mm vívoactive 4S GPS smartwatch. It has the broadest available range of all-day health monitoring features, music storage and on-screen, Animated workouts To help you reach your goals. Boost every activity by downloading your favorite Spotify® or Deezer (may require a premium subscription With a third-party music provider) playlists straight to your watch.
features: Keeps track of your energy levels, Pulse Ox (this is not a medical device and is not intended for use in the diagnosis or monitoring of any medical condition), respiration, menstrual cycle, stress, sleep, estimated heart, hydration and more

Easily download songs to your watch, including playlists from Spotify, Amazon music or Deezer (may require a premium subscription With a third-party music provider), and connect with headphones (sold separately) for phone-free listening
Record all the ways to move with more than 20 preloaded GPS and indoor sports apps, including yoga, running, swimming and more. Display resolution:218 x 218 pixels
Get easy-to-follow, animated workouts right on your watch screen, including strength, cardio, yoga and Pilates
Battery life: Up to 7 days in smartwatch mode; Up to 5 hours in GPS and music mode
Personalize your watch with thousands of free watch faces, apps and widgets from our Connect IQ store
Garmin Pay contactless payment solution (not all countries and payment Networks are eligible) lets you pay for purchases with your watch
Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) and assistance (when paired with a compatible device), which both send your real-time location to emergency contacts
Stay connected with smart notifications (when paired with a compatible device), for incoming calls, text messages, calendar reminders and more
Garmin Coach provides free 5K, 10K and half-marathon training plans that guide you to reach your race goal and adjust based on your performance in the plan

reviews.title: Everything and more from what i expected from a smart watch
reviews.text: i spent a great deal of time researching smart watches, Garmin came up the winner.  I have dealt with their customer service on two occasions with another product and found them to be excellent which i a big reason for buying another Garmin product.

reviews.title: Poor battery life, feature rich
reviews.text:
If only the battery life was better... I have the GPS turned off and I only measure my blood oxygen level when I'm asleep yet it is nearly impossible for the watch to last a little more than 24 hours.

My next Garmin will be the one with a solar powered assist.

That being said for the negatives this watch has the features I wanted at a good price:

- Sleep quality/quantity
- Blood oxygen level
- Steps counter
- Flight of stairs counting
- Water intake recording ) manual input)
- Multiple activities recording (it can detect when I'm running, bike riding, etc.)

It has been durable and the screen is still scratch free.

If poor battery life isn't a deal breaker buy it.

reviews.title: After a year and a half...can't wait to switch, Spotify is the worst part of this watch by far
reviews.text:
I have owned this watch for a year and a half now. I use it almost daily for my walks/runs, weight workouts, and indoor bike workouts. The main reason I chose this watch in the first place was because it was one of the smaller ones available. I am 5'9 but have small wrists, and really disliked the 'utilitarian' look of larger watches. After a year and a half, I am so tired of the issues with it and ready to jump ship. If I used this watch more regularly (I only wear it for workouts - not all day), I would have switched a LONG time ago. The amount of people who have complained about the same issues for YEARS without Garmin doing anything makes me hesitant to purchase another Garmin product, especially with the Apple Watch, Samsung, and now Google buying out Fitbit and coming up with a watch.
- Spotify on this watch seriously sucks. If you check Spotify forums, you will find COUNTLESS threads of suggestions for the Spotify app on this watch that should truly be basic features at this price. Do they implement them or listen? No. Also, there is no way to contact Spotify customer service - you just get pointed towards their 'community forum' where you have people complaining of the same exact things with no fix. You can't skip forward on any track, at all. I listen to a lot of podcasts when I walk. Some are 2 hours in length while my walk is only 1 hour. This watch constantly either reverts to the very beginning of my podcast so I have to start all over (because again, you can't skip forward - so have fun listening to ads as well!) in anything, only updates 1 song out of 15 on my playlist or get the error 'sync failed', or some other random issue. Also, if you have a playlist on Spotify, you cannot choose the track you want to listen to. You have to hit the >> button to get to where you want. Really? I hope Garmin drops Spotify because they have been a truly horrible app on this watch the entire time with no effort to make updates. Take a look at the reviews of Spotify on the Garmin store if you don't believe me.
- Battery life is a couple of days. However, there are points in time where my watch will be at say 80% the night before, I get ready to leave and go my walk the next morning, pick up my watch, and it's at 8%. WTF? This happens enough to be super annoying and apparently is not an 'only me' problem. It appears to be tied to when the watch updates.
- I tried to wear this to track my sleep in the beginning and gave up after two nights due to the extreme disco effect of the 'gesture' motions. I tried turning on the 'do not disturb' function advertised on the watch which is supposed to disable this stupidly bright light that WILL wake you and whoever is next to you up. Didn't work. Do a search for 'Garmin watch lights up in middle of the night' and you will find all sorts of people complaining of the same thing, and not just on this version of the watch.
- GPS has issues connecting and can take several minutes which is pretty annoying when you try to start tracking an activity only to see a red bar when trying to connect. I 'sync' my watch to my phone constantly so that is not the issue. I have switched between the GPS options Garmin offers on the watch to no avail. Search for this issue as well and you will see lots of people with the same complaint.

reviews.title: GET THIS WATCH!!!
reviews.text: I LOVE this watch!!! It is so easy to use, tracks all of the metrics I am interested in (steps, sleep, stress, activities, hydration, menstrual cycle) and then some!! It is very accurate and the app is very user- friendly. The watch itself has worked perfectly and I have found it to be comfortable and intuitive to use.  It holds a charge for several days on end and when it does need to be charged, it reaches full battery very quickly! I looked at a lot of different watches and considered a lot of different options. I am so happy that I went with the vivoactive 4! Garmin, you all did a great job with this watch!! I honestly could not be happier with my choice!!

reviews.title: A Gift that went well
reviews.text:
This was a Christmas gift for my wife to replace her older Vivosmart. She wanted the new features but she has a small wrist.
Doing some searches I thought this would be as big as I should try.

All went well. It does all she needs and while it's much better it's not too big. Doing larger does add more battery time and more features, but this is the right fit for her
